rationale: Both notes center on the same core domain—consumer evaluation of distant/moderately dissimilar brand extensions and perceived fit—and the cited paper provides a situational consumer-state mechanism (mating mind-set) with boundary conditions (including subbrand architecture) that closely overlap with the citing paper’s theorizing and moderators. The citing note explicitly says it extends prior work on consumer-state determinants of extension evaluation, so this citation is plausibly a substantive theory/comparison source rather than an incidental mention.
